title: Vendor-agnostic data routing + fallback layer for the multi-asset data path
description: TradingAgents' dataflows/interface.py route_to_vendor() pattern: category/tool-level routing with a primary-vendor -> fallback chain (e.g. on rate-limit). As QuantHack goes XAU-D1 -> multi-asset H4 (current feat/phase5-m3-v3.2 work), a thin routing layer over MT5/FRED/etc. with fallback would cut per-asset plumbing. Lower priority than the reflection log (B0051) and alt-data source (B0052), but lands right where the multi-asset expansion is being built.
source: Analysis of D:\PROJECTS\TradingAgents-main (Tauric Research multi-agent LLM trading framework) for transferable patterns, 2026-05-27. Most of it is LLM-as-trader (forbidden by our rule-based-primary contract + lookahead firewall), but three engineering patterns transfer.
context: Relevant to the in-flight multi-asset program (B0050 / piece #3/#4). Pure engineering convenience, no edge claim. Capture only — triage to a B-ID if multi-asset sourcing friction actually shows up.
